calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset And Petition For Instructions.
Off Calendar Apr-01-2011 Text Ruling

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset And Petition For Instructions.
Off Calendar Dec-01-2010 Continued To Feb-18-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Piccinini for and with Donna Fappiano and for Pual Fappiano (Respondents). Robert Peterson for and with Twila Parker (Successor-Trustee).

Mr. Peterson stated that the matter involves an issue of another Trust, that they did not resolve the issue, that there was no opposition to the Heggstad petition to have the asset declared part of the Trust, that Mr. Piccinini filed a statement but no objection, and that the filing fee for an objection was not paid. Mr. Piccinini stated that the statement was an objection, that there was no accounting for the income and distribution, that there was an issue of the Cat Gabby, that the Fappiano's have provided care for the cat and that they were entitled to compensation. The Court informed Mr. Piccinini that he needed to file an opposition to the petition so that the pleadings can be at issue and then the matter can be set. If no opposition is filed, the Court will bifurcate the issue of the 850 (Heggstad) petition and take the matter under submission. Trial on the remaining issues was set for February 18, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset And Petition For Instructions.
Off Calendar Sep-29-2010 Continued To Dec-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Piccinini for Donna Fappiano and Paul Fappiono. Robert Peterson for and with Twila Parker (Petitioner).

The Parties stipulated to a continuance stating that they were working on settlement. The matter was continued to December 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset And Petition For Instructions.
Off Calendar Aug-25-2010 Continued To Sep-29-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Piccinini for and with Donna Fappiano and Paul Fappiono (Beneficiaries). Robert Peterson for and with Twila Parker.

Mr. Peterson stated that his position is that the Second Amendment/First Amendment is clear that when the cat Aladdin dies, that the trust is to be distributed. The matter was continued to September 29, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and setting the matter for trial.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
